title: Speaking - Part 2
description: Compare the photographs and answer the questions.

tasks.instructions: Compare two of the pictures, and say what the lifestyle advantages and disadvantages of different types of housing might be and who might prefer each.

images.label: A
images.description: A row of colourful terraced houses on a quiet suburban street, each with a small front garden and cars parked outside

images.label: B
images.description: A modern high-rise apartment block in a busy city centre, with balconies overlooking a vibrant street full of shops and cafés

images.label: C
images.description: An isolated farmhouse surrounded by open fields and woodland, with a vegetable garden and a dirt track leading to the main road

followUp.question: Which type of housing do you think offers the best quality of life, and why?

tasks.instructions: Compare two of the pictures, and say what challenges people might face when moving to a new place and how they might adapt to their new environment.

images.label: D
images.description: A young couple unpacking cardboard boxes in a small, unfurnished flat, looking both excited and exhausted

images.label: E
images.description: A family arriving at a new city with suitcases, looking at a map on their phone while surrounded by unfamiliar streets and signs

images.label: F
images.description: A man standing alone outside a new house in a quiet neighbourhood, looking at his new home with a thoughtful, slightly uncertain expression

followUp.question: Do you think it is easier to move to a new city or a new country? What makes one harder than the other?
